title: Anatomy of a Lactococcal Phage Tail
summary: The paper focuses on the Siphoviridae family phages, particularly Tuc2009 and TP901-1, detailing their tail structures, host specificity, and morphogenesis. Tuc2009 exhibits a tail structure similar to λ, with a baseplate involved in host recognition and a collar structure at the head-tail interface. The study also compares these phages to other lactococcal phages like ul36, LC3, and r1t, highlighting genetic and structural similarities and differences.

Tuc2009.morphology: small isometric head, long noncontractile tail, baseplate, collar structure, whisker-like appendages
Tuc2009.structural_proteins: Orf45 (MTP), Orf49 (Dit), Orf50 (Tal), Orf51 (BppU), Orf52 (BppA), Orf53 (BppL), Orf55 (NPS)
Tuc2009.growth_medium: M17 medium supplemented with 0.5% glucose (GM17) or glucose streptococcal broth medium

extra.baseplate_structure: single disk with teardrop-like appurtenances, conical structure with protruding fiber

λ.morphology: isometric head, long noncontractile tail, baseplate
